summary: Matched Kirby's copied Fox/Falco neutral-special blaster code in ftkirbyspecialfox.c, including ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 and small closures in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240. The PR added needed includes and header externs, replaced an unmatched stub/wrapper with C, duplicated a blaster-id switch at call sites, and review focused on avoiding fake-looking goto control flow and preserving OSReport/HSD_ASSERT macro usage.

key_files.role: Primary matched source file; implemented ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30, adjusted blaster setup in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240, added includes, and removed a local helper.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported 3 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialfox

key_files.role: Header surface updated with extern declarations for data used by ftkirbyspecialfox.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added ftKb_Init_804D3DB8, ftKb_Init_804D3DC0, ftKb_Init_804D9478, ftKb_Init_804D9480 externs

what_changed.text: Implemented ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 where the file previously had an unmatched marker and a wrapper-style ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed /// #ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 and added void ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30(Fighter_GObj* gobj)
text_corpus: bot report lists ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 from 0.00% to 100.00%, +432 bytes

what_changed.text: Kept ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot as a wrapper that calls ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 after moving the recovered body into ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30.
Added launch-position calculation using Vec3 locals, lb_8000B1CC on FtPart_R3rdNa, and fallback zero-vector initialization, then forces sp2C.z to 0.
evidence_refs: review comment r2983173424: author showed the goto-less conditional form

what_changed.text: Added Fox/Falco switch handling for launch angle, launch speed, projectile id, blaster laser cleanup, and SFX table selection.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: switch on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30

what_changed.text: Removed inline ftKbGetBlasterId helper and duplicated its Fox/Falco blaster item id switch in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added local u32 blaster_id and switch in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100
raw_diff: added local u32 blaster_id and switch in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240

what_changed.text: Added includes for ft/ft_0877.h and it/items/itfoxlaser.h.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include additions near top of ftkirbyspecialfox.c

what_changed.text: Moved ftKb_Init_804D9478 and ftKb_Init_804D9480 extern declarations from the C file into ftKb_Init.h and added additional data externs for SFX tables.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed local extern const f32 ftKb_Init_804D9478 and ftKb_Init_804D9480

what_changed.text: Automated report claimed matched-code progress of +0.03%, +1040 bytes, with three new function matches and one .sdata2 improvement.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot issue comment at 2026-03-24T16:10:54Z

smart_moves.text: The author responded to review by pushing a goto-less version for the contentious conditional sequence.
evidence_refs: review comment r2984086278: author says 'aight, I pushed the goto-less version'
raw_diff: final visible ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 uses if/else rather than the reviewed goto label

smart_moves.text: Header externs were centralized in ftKb_Init.h instead of leaving at least the float externs source-local.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed local extern const f32 declarations from ftkirbyspecialfox.c

smart_moves.text: The final diff preserved readable OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage in visible hunks after reviewer objected to raw __assert/string-symbol replacements.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: visible final hunks do not include the reviewed OSReport-to-symbol or HSD_ASSERT-to-__assert replacement

decomp_lessons.text: When a match is achieved with generated-looking goto control flow and reviewers identify it as probably not authored source, try a structured if/else form if it also matches.
evidence_refs: review comment r2982940692: reviewer calls the goto version a fake match
review comment r2983173424: author provides a less-fake if/else alternative
review comment r2984086278: author pushed the goto-less version

decomp_lessons.text: PAD_STACK may be tolerated as a temporary matching tactic, but this review did not treat it as something worth documenting in-source as a fake-match warning.
evidence_refs: review comment r2983173424: author notes PAD_STACK is fake by strict definition but allowed for now
review comment r2983620969: reviewer says PAD_STACK appears throughout the repo and will likely be cleaned up near end of decomp

decomp_lessons.text: Do not regress established OSReport/HSD_ASSERT source forms to raw __assert calls or string-symbol arguments just to satisfy matching pressure.
evidence_refs: review comment r2984311199: 'Would prefer we don't undo proper OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage.'
review comment r2984313189: same feedback repeated

decomp_lessons.text: Inlining/factoring hypotheses can be match-sensitive: the author suspected fp2 implied an inlined helper but reported that factoring it out did not restore the match.

naming_conventions.text: Address-style data names such as ftKb_Init_804D3DB8, ftKb_Init_804D3DC0, ftKb_Init_804D9478, and ftKb_Init_804D9480 were retained rather than renamed semantically.
Short local role names were used for common decomp entities: fp, fp2, da, sp2C, pos1, blaster_id, and blasterGObj.
Function names stayed in the existing ftKb_SpecialN* address/behavior naming scheme rather than introducing new semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used PAD_STACK(4) in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 as a narrow stack-shape tactic.
evidence_refs: review comments r2983173424 and r2983620969 discuss PAD_STACK

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used f64 launchAngle and M_PI minus the per-character launch angle when facing direction is not 1.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: f64 launchAngle and M_PI - da->specialn_*_launch_angle branches

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Selected SFX table entries using the boolean expression [-1 == fp->facing_dir] as an index.
Duplicated the Fox/Falco blaster-id switch at two call sites after removing an inline helper, indicating the helper abstraction did not match the desired codegen.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added switch in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Tried a suspected helper factoring path but abandoned it when it failed to restore the match.
evidence_refs: review comment r2983173424: author says factoring out suspected inline did not restore match

review_feedback.text: Initial reviewer believed the goto version was a fake match and suggested including a decomp.me scratch comment or a fake-match comment if it could not be solved.
Author questioned the boundary of 'fake match', citing M2C_FIELD, comma assignments, and PAD_STACK, and proposed a less-fake structured conditional that still worked.
Reviewer clarified the goto was considered fake because the original source likely did not use a goto; the same reviewer did not think PAD_STACK needed special mention yet.
Another reviewer preferred the cleaner goto-less option and said a fake-match comment was not necessarily useful when the questionable shape was obvious.

evidence_refs: raw_diff: final visible conditional is goto-less

review_feedback.text: Reviewer requested not undoing proper OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage; the same concern was repeated for a second hunk.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, counts, changed files
text_corpus: bot report and review comment records, truncated after visible records
review_comments: loaded excerpt truncated near final repeated OSReport/HSD_ASSERT comment
notes: Changed files and final diff are strong because raw_diff is loaded untruncated.
Automated match results are based on the decomp-dev bot issue comment; no independent build, objdiff, or regression logs were run during intake.
Review evidence is mixed because the review_comments and text_corpus loaded files are truncated, though the key fake-match/goto and OSReport/HSD_ASSERT discussions are visible.
The final diff excerpt supports that the visible goto was removed, but it does not prove every reviewer concern was fully resolved outside the shown hunks.
No claim is made about original developer intent beyond reviewer/author statements and the final code shape.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Goto-shaped fake matches should be challenged when a structured equivalent also matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2349, reviewers objected to a goto-based match in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 as likely fake; the author supplied and then pushed a goto-less if/else form. This supports a searchable lesson to try structured control flow before accepting generated-looking goto shapes.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve report/assert macros during matching cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2349 review explicitly objected to undoing proper OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage in favor of raw string-symbol and __assert forms.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Helper abstraction may be removed when duplicated switch code matches better
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR removed inline ftKbGetBlasterId and duplicated its Fox/Falco blaster-id switch in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240, indicating the helper abstraction did not fit the recovered matching source in this case.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed ftKbGetBlasterId and added local blaster_id switches

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify fake-match comments versus cleanup action
source_update_candidates.text: Review discussion in PR #2349 suggests that when a questionable generated-looking control-flow shape is obvious, reviewers may prefer trying a cleaner matching form over adding a 'fake match' comment; a scratch/comment may still be useful if no cleaner solution is found.
source_update_candidates.reason: This may refine existing canonical-control-flow or matching-tactics guidance, but it should be curated rather than promoted directly from one PR.

source_update_candidates.title: ftkirbyspecialfox.c matched functions in PR #2349
source_update_candidates.text: The automated report for PR #2349 lists ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30, ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100, and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 as reaching 100% in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialfox.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-level historical match-progress fact; intake did not run verification.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that PAD_STACK is accepted permanent style from this PR; discussion frames it as tolerated for now and likely cleanup later.
Do not infer original source used the final exact conditional or duplicated switches; the PR evidence only supports that these forms were used to match and pass review.
Do not promote semantic meanings for address-style ftKb_Init_804D* symbols beyond their observed use in the diff.
Do not claim full validation beyond the loaded decomp-dev bot report; no intake build or objdiff was run.
